content: Cardiac rehabilitation (CR) is a comprehensive, multidisciplinary program designed to optimize cardiovascular health in patients with established CVD. Core components: (1) medically supervised exercise training, (2) cardiovascular risk factor modification, (3) psychosocial and vocational counseling, (4) patient education. Indications (Class I): post-MI, stable angina, post-PCI, post-CABG, heart valve repair/replacement, heart transplant/LVAD, stable HFrEF. Phases: Phase I (inpatient—early mobilization), Phase II (outpatient supervised—typically 36 sessions over 12 weeks, ECG monitored initially), Phase III (maintenance—supervised but less intensive), Phase IV (independent—long-term maintenance). Benefits: reduces all-cause mortality ~20-25%, CV mortality ~26%, hospital readmissions, improves functional capacity (VO2 max increases 15-30%), quality of life, psychological well-being. Mechanisms: improved endothelial function, autonomic balance, skeletal muscle metabolism, medication adherence.
